summary:
The provided information does NOT show that residency selection criteria can predict surgical performance due to limitations in measuring essential non-trainable traits like conscientiousness and work ethic at time of selection. Instead, these foundational characteristics emerge early and depend significantly on post-entry supervisor engagement—which occurs after formal admissions. Thus, prediction based solely on selection metrics lacks validity since actual outcomes rely on dynamic interactions within the program environment rather than static applicant profiles.

The summary should reflect only what was directly stated in the text above; avoid adding external knowledge or interpretation.

Only two sentences:

Residency selection criteria do not predict performance because vital personal traits like conscientiousness cannot be assessed during selection and remain outside measurement scope.  
These traits instead evolve under ongoing supervision, meaning success hinges on post-admission mentoring relationships rather than upfront screening processes.
